targetSequence.desc: Write 1: SET latch, read gate closed
Idle: hold state, read gate closed
Write 0: RESET latch, read gate closed

hint: Inputs are [DataIn, WriteEnable, ReadEnable]. Write path: S = DataIn AND WE, R = NOT(DataIn) AND WE → feed to SR Latch. Read path: Out0 = Q AND RE, Out1 = Q̄ AND RE. When WE=0, S=R=0 (hold). When RE=0, outputs are gated to 0.
title: Register File Cell
objective: Build a 1-bit register cell with read/write enable
description: Build the fundamental building block of a register file: a 1-bit storage cell with separate Write Enable (WE) and Read Enable (RE) control. The write path derives Set/Reset from DataIn and WE. The read path gates the stored value through RE. This is exactly how register files in real CPUs are constructed — one cell per bit, arrayed into registers.
